source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/7167_Passage 1 Chocolate for the masses.html

passages.title: Chocolate for the masses
passages.source_html:
Chocolate for the masses


A


For almost three thousand years, chocolate was a drink of the elite and the wealthy, originally in South America and later on in Europe. In the early 19th century, however, chocolate became far more widely available, and consumption shot up dramatically across the populations of Europe in particular.


B 


It was 1828 that marked the beginning of the modern era in chocolate making and production. Chocolate as a drink had been known for centuries, but in 1828 a Dutch chemist named Van Houten developed a process for the manufacture of a new kind of powdered chocolate using a hydraulic press. Untreated chocolate 'liquor' – the end result of grinding the cacao beans from which chocolate is obtained – contains about 53 per cent cacao butter,  but this machine removed nearly half of this, leaving a 'cake' that could be pulverized into a fine powder – what is now known as 'cocoa'.


C 


In 1847, the British firm Fry and Sons found a way to mix a blend of cocoa powder and sugar with melted cacao butter to produce a thin paste which could be cast into various shapes. This was the world's first true solid chocolate. Thanks to immediate high demand for this product, the price of cacao butter escalated, so the new solid chocolate was only accessible for the elite. But this would eventually change, because of the introduction of cost-cutting methods and mass production.


D


By the latter half of the 19th century, many manufacturers had begun making their own chocolate, and using cocoa powder to hand-coat sugar confections. Cocoa powder also reached wide use in many other products, like ice creams and biscuits. Entrepreneurial families like the Frys and Cadburys in Britain had a social conscience in the midst of all this money-making, unlike many other 19th-century captains of industry. In the Birmingham suburb of Bournville, where they had established their chocolate factory, the Cadburys created a model town with adequate housing for their workers, and even dining and reading rooms so that their employees had no need to spend anything on entertaining themselves.


E 


However, the rising demand for chocolate also made it a target for unscrupulous producers and merchants. Sometimes the expensive cacao butter was completely extracted and replaced with olive or almond oil, or egg yolks. Alternatively, cheaper materials such as potato starch or flour were added. In 1850, a health commission was created in Britain for the analysis of foods. Suspicions about chocolate proved well-founded – most of the samples contained starch grains from potatoes or other plants. The investigation inspired the British Food and Drug Act of 1860 and the Adulteration of Food Act of 1872.


F 


The invention of milk chocolate took place in Switzerland, and was a collaboration between two men. The first was Henri Nestle, a chemist who in 1867 discovered a process to make powdered milk by evaporation, when mixed with water, this could be fed to infants and small children. The second man, Daniel Peter, came up with the idea of using Nestle's milk powder in the manufacture of a new kind of chocolate, and, in 1879, the first milk chocolate bar was produced.


G 


Also in 1879 another Swiss, Rodolphe Lindt, invented the conche machine, which vastly improved the quality of chocolate confectionery. Before Lindt, solid chocolate was usually coarse and gritty. Now, however, it achieved such a degree of smoothness that Lindt named it 'fondant', after the smooth sugar creams of that name. People on both sides of the Atlantic developed a taste for fondant chocolate, and the use of the conche machine for solid chocolate became universal in the business.


H 


In the USA, Milton Hershey dominated the chocolate industry. In 1893, after seeing examples of the machinery used in Europe in action at an international exhibition in Chicago, he bought some and began turning out chocolate coatings for the caramels he was already producing. But after a trip to the chocolate centers of Europe, he sold his caramel business for a million dollars, bought a farm in Pennsylvania, and built a chocolate factory there.


I 


This became the nucleus of 'Hershey, the Chocolate Town', which contained amongst other things Hershey's imposing mansion, the Hershey Department Store and the Hershey Bank. There was, however, no elected mayor or indeed any democratic system in place. The whole town was in essence Milton Hershey's private kingdom, and he ran it as he wanted.


J 


This was not the only Hershey town in existence. During a trip which he made to the island of Cuba in 1915, Hershey was inspired to build a new model town centered round a mill for grinding sugar. To transport the refined sugar so that it could be shipped by sea to his chocolate and cocoa factory, Hershey built modern electric railroads too.


K 


To help advertise his products, Hershey employed nutritionists to proclaim their health qualities, and his chocolate bars and cocoa soon commanded the American market. Everything was mechanized – a true assembly-line operation. Hershey's best-selling bar contained almonds imported from southern Europe, dropped by machines into the waiting molds. Another popular product was 'Hershey's Kisses' – bite-sized, flat-bottomed drops of chocolate, individually wrapped.


L 


Nowadays so many tourists visit Hershey that the company no longer offers tours of its factory. Rather, visitors are whisked along on automated carts through an exhibition called 'Chocolate World', where they can see how their favorite products are produced.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

Chocolate was consumed by greater numbers of people in the nineteenth century than in previous times.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是“19世纪比以前有更多人消费巧克力”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“19th century”和“greater numbers of people”


对应原文：A段，“In the early 19th century, however, chocolate became far more widely available, and consumption shot up dramatically across the populations of Europe in particular.”


解题思路：原文明确提到“19世纪初期，巧克力变得更加广泛可得，消费量在欧洲人口中急剧上升”。这说明19世纪的巧克力消费量比之前多。

2

Fry's chocolate became more affordable because of the fall in price of one ingredient.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是“Fry的巧克力因为某种成分的降价而变得更加便宜”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“Fry’s chocolate”和“affordable”


对应原文：C段，“Thanks to immediate high demand for this product, the price of cacao butter escalated, so the new solid chocolate was only accessible for the elite. But this would eventually change, because of the introduction of cost-cutting methods and mass production.”


解题思路：

原文提到巧克力的巨大需求导致其成分cacao butter的价格上涨（escalated），这导致只有精英阶层买得起巧克力。随后巧克力变得更大众化，并不是因为哪种原料价格下跌，而是因为采用了降低成本的方法和规模化生产。

3

Entrepreneurial British chocolate manufacturers paid their employees well.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是“英国的巧克力制造商给员工支付了很高的工资”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“British chocolate manufacturers”和“paid their employees well”


对应原文：D段，“In the Birmingham suburb of Bournville, where they had established their chocolate factory, the Cadburys created a model town with adequate housing for their workers, and even dining and reading rooms so that their employees had no need to spend anything on entertaining themselves.”


解题思路：原文提到的是Cadbury公司为员工提供了住房和休闲设施，但没有提到工资支付情况。因此无法判断他们是否支付了高工资。

4

Customers were made ill through the practices of unscrupulous chocolate producers.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是“不良巧克力生产商的行为导致消费者生病”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“made ill”和“unscrupulous chocolate producers”


对应原文：E段提到，“However, the rising demand for chocolate also made it a target for unscrupulous producers and merchants. Sometimes the expensive cacao butter was completely extracted and replaced with olive or almond oil, or egg yolks. Alternatively, cheaper materials such as potato starch or flour were added. In 1850, a health commission was created in Britain for the analysis of foods Suspicions about chocolate proved well-founded-most of the samples contained starch grains from potatoes or other plants.”


解题思路：原文虽然提到了一些不良行为，但没有提到因此导致顾客生病的信息。因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

5

Fresh milk was used in the production of milk chocolate in Switzerland.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是“在瑞士，生产牛奶巧克力时使用了新鲜牛奶”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“Fresh milk”、“used”、“production of milk chocolate”、“Switzerland”



对应原文：F段，“The invention of milk chocolate took place in Switzerland, and was a collaboration between two men. The first was Henri Nestle, a chemist who in 1867 discovered a process to make powdered milk by evaporation, when mixed with water, this could be fed to infants and small children. The second man, Daniel Peter, came up with the idea of using Nestle's milk powder in the manufacture of a new kind of chocolate, and, in 1879, the first milk chocolate bar was produced.”


解题思路：原文提到的是使用奶粉（milk powder），而不是新鲜牛奶（fresh milk）。因此题目说法错误。

6

Lindt's conche machine was adopted by other manufacturers.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是“Lindt的精炼机被其他制造商采用”是否正确。


定位关键信息：“Lindt’s conche machine”


对应原文：G段，“People on both sides of the Atlantic developed a taste for fondant chocolate, and the use of the conche machine for solid chocolate became universal in the business.”


解题思路：原文明确提到Lindt的精炼机在巧克力行业内被广泛使用（became universal）。因此，说明被其他制造商采用。

Questions 7 - 13


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

7

Milton Hershey and the chocolate industry


• Hershey bought some after visiting a trade fair.

正确答案： 7 machinery
Question 7

定位关键词：

bought，after visiting a trade fair


定位原文：H段，“In 1893, after seeing examples of the machinery used in Europe in action at an international exhibition in Chicago, he bought some and began turning out chocolate coatings for the caramels he was already producing.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey在参观展会后购买了一些机器（machinery）。

8

• After selling his caramel business, Hershey purchased a .

正确答案： 8 farm
Question 8

定位关键词：

caramel business


定位原文：H段，“he sold his caramel business for a million dollars, bought a farm in Pennsylvania, and built a chocolate factory there.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey卖掉了焦糖业务后购买了农场（farm）。

9

• The inhabitants of Hershey could not vote for a .

正确答案： 9 mayor
Question 9

定位关键词：

inhabitants，vote


定位原文：I段，“There was, however, no elected mayor or indeed any democratic system in place.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey镇没有市长（mayor），居民无法选举市长。

10

• Hershey set up a new town in Cuba around a mill.

• Hershey constructed to transport material.

正确答案： 10 railroads
Question 10

定位关键词：

transport material


定位原文：J段，“To transport the refined sugar so that it could be shipped by sea to his chocolate and cocoa factory, Hershey built modern electric railroads too.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey修建了铁路（railroads）来运输材料。

11

• Hershey brought in to improve the image of his chocolate.

正确答案： 11 nutritionists
Question 11

定位关键词：

improve the image of his chocolate


定位原文：K段，“Hershey employed nutritionists to proclaim their health qualities.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey雇佣了营养学家（nutritionists）来宣传巧克力的健康形象。

12

• One popular chocolate product had added to it.

正确答案： 12 almonds
Question 12

定位关键词：

popular，add


定位原文：K段，“Hershey's best-selling bar contained almonds imported from southern Europe.”


解题思路：原文提到Hershey的热销巧克力产品加入了杏仁（almonds）。

13

• Because of the huge demand, tourists can only visit the at Hershey.

正确答案： 13 exhibition
Question 13

定位关键词：

the huge demand, tourists


定位原文：L段，“Rather, visitors are whisked along on automated carts through an exhibition called 'Chocolate World'.”


解题思路：原文提到游客只能参观“展览”（exhibition），而不是工厂。
